title: Energy storage device with a first metal layer formed from a precursor layer upon charge and diffused into a cathode during discharge
abstract: Embodiments of the present invention are in the field of materials, apparatus, process, methods, and designs for manufacture of a thin film energy storage devices with a capacity greater then 1 mA-hr-cmâ2 including thin film Lithium metal and Li+ ion batteries and capacitors having high energy density and high cycle life due to the incorporation of at least one vacuum thin film with respect to protection and electrical conductivity of the electrodes, and at least one vacuum thin film electrolyte for electrical insulation of the electrodes and ion conduction after assembly for low self discharge and high cycle life battery cells.
claims: 1. A method of forming a battery cell, comprising: providing for one or more layers between a first electrode structure and a second electrode structure containing an ion material, the one or more layers including an electrolyte precursor layer with a planarized electrical insulating ion conducting layer; and applying a voltage between the first electrode structure and the second electrode structure to provide ion flow from the second electrode structure to the first electrode structure through the electrolyte precursor layer, form an ion conducting electrolyte from the electrolyte precursor layer during the ion flow, deposit a layer of metal on the first electrode structure to form an anode where the first electrode structure and the one or more thin-film layers form a current collector electrode, and form a cathode from the second electrode structure.
